Title: A flavin-dependent monooxygenase produces nitrogenous tomato aroma volatiles using cysteine as a nitrogen source
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: M82; Large Red Cherry; Pearson; Vendor
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Flavin-dependent monooxygenase (FMO), clade VI; group A-like FMO with FAD-binding motifs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cysteine-derived tetrahydrothiazolidine route to nitriles, nitro compounds, aldoximes, and thiazoles
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 2-substituted tetrahydrothiazolidine-4-carboxylic acids, including 2-isobutyltetrahydrothiazolidine-4-carboxylic acid and 2-benzyl-1,3-thiazolidine-4-carboxylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: N-hydroxylated tetrahydrothiazolidine intermediate(s); downstream products include corresponding aldoxime, nitro, nitrile volatiles and thiazole
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sequential N-hydroxylation of cysteine-derived 2-substituted tetrahydrothiazolidine-4-carboxylic acids, enabling formation of nitrogenous tomato aroma volatiles
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for production of multiple terminal nitrogenous volatiles from cysteine-derived conjugates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential pathway enzyme; key control point for detectable nitrogenous volatile production in ripe tomato fruit
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to tomato aroma and flavor quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing or stop-codon disruption abolishes or strongly reduces all detectable aliphatic and aromatic nitrogenous volatiles in ripe tomato fruit; heterologous expression enables conversion of supplied thiazolidines into corresponding nitrogenous volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL fine mapping; antisense silencing in tomato; EMS stop-codon variant analysis; untargeted UPLC-QTOF-MS metabolomics; stable isotope feeding; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low before ripening; induced at breaker and highest in ripe fruit; much lower in leaves and flowers; low in S. pennellii ripe fruit and chromosome 12 introgression lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ripening-associated fruit expression; ethylene inducible in mature green fruit and reduced by 1-MCP
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct from previously known plant FMOs and defines clade VI; accepts substrates derived from both L-cysteine and D-cysteine; cysteamine-derived substrates lacking the 4-carboxyl group were not converted; product profile indicates sequential N-hydroxylations; a putative N-hydroxy-IT4C intermediate was detected
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Related phenylalanine- and leucine-derived alcohols and aldehydes were not affected in silenced lines; no significant GABA difference reported
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Antisense construct in pHKAS, promoter NA; transient expression construct in pHKOE/pBIN61 system, promoter NA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted metabolomics (UPLC-QTOF-MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering or breeding tomato flavor by restoring or increasing nitrogenous volatile production; useful marker for favorable aroma alleles, with potential stacking with other flavor genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc12g013690 was fine-mapped from a chromosome 12 QTL controlling nitrogenous volatiles in tomato fruit. 2. Antisense suppression and an EMS stop-codon variant greatly reduced or abolished nitrogenous volatile accumulation. 3. Metabolomics identified IT4C accumulation in loss-of-function backgrounds, indicating a cysteine-derived substrate. 4. Stable isotope feeding demonstrated that cysteine supplies the nitrogen atom for tomato nitrogenous volatiles. 5. Transient expression showed the enzyme converts thiazolidine substrates into nitrile, nitro, oxime, and thiazole volatiles, establishing it as tetrahydrothiazolidine N-hydroxylase.
